AC.L2-3.1.1.proof: SSP documents access control policies. System access restricted to authorized users via Active Directory group policies. Access control lists reviewed quarterly.

AC.L2-3.1.2.proof: Transaction and function restrictions implemented via RBAC. SSP documents permitted functions per role. Application-level access controls enforce authorized transactions.

AC.L2-3.1.3.proof: CUI flow documented in SSP for primary systems. Data flow diagrams exist but not comprehensive for all CUI-handling systems.
AC.L2-3.1.3.plan: Complete CUI data flow mapping for all systems in scope. Implement network controls to enforce approved CUI flow paths. Add to POAM.

AC.L2-3.1.4.proof: Separation of duties implemented for system administration and security functions. Not fully documented or enforced across all CUI-handling processes.
AC.L2-3.1.4.plan: Document SoD requirements for all CUI-processing roles. Implement automated SoD checks in access provisioning.

AC.L2-3.1.5.proof: Least privilege principle documented in SSP. Privileged accounts limited to authorized administrators. Privileged access reviewed quarterly per NIST 800-171 requirement.

AC.L2-3.1.6.proof: Standard user accounts used for non-security functions. Separate admin accounts required for privileged operations. Policy enforced via GPO and documented in SSP.

AC.L2-3.1.7.proof: Privileged function execution restricted to admin accounts. Audit logging captures all privileged function executions. UAC enabled on all Windows endpoints. Sudo logging on Linux systems.

AC.L2-3.1.8.proof: Account lockout policy configured: 5 failed attempts triggers 30-minute lockout. Policy enforced via AD GPO across all domain-joined systems. Documented in SSP.

AC.L2-3.1.9.proof: System use notification banners deployed on all CUI systems. Banner language reviewed by legal and includes DoD-required CUI handling notice.

AC.L2-3.1.10.proof: Screen lock configured at 15-minute inactivity timeout via GPO. Pattern-hiding display activated. Documented in SSP and validated during internal assessment.

AC.L2-3.1.11.proof: Session termination configured for inactive sessions after 30 minutes. VPN sessions terminated after 8 hours of inactivity. Settings documented in SSP.

AC.L2-3.1.12.proof: Remote access via VPN only. VPN sessions logged and monitored. Remote access policy documented in SSP. Multi-factor authentication required for all remote sessions.

AC.L2-3.1.13.proof: VPN uses FIPS-validated cryptographic modules (AES-256). Remote access sessions encrypted end-to-end. Cryptographic implementation documented in SSP.

AC.L2-3.1.14.proof: Remote access routed through managed access control points (VPN concentrator). Direct remote connections prohibited. Network architecture documented in SSP.

AC.L2-3.1.15.proof: Remote privileged commands require VPN plus MFA. Authorization process exists but not formally documented in SSP for all privileged command scenarios.
AC.L2-3.1.15.plan: Document all authorized remote privileged command scenarios in SSP. Implement session recording for remote privileged access.

AC.L2-3.1.16.proof: Wireless access requires pre-authorization and WPA3-Enterprise authentication. Guest wireless network isolated. Authorization process partially documented.
AC.L2-3.1.16.plan: Complete wireless access authorization procedures documentation in SSP.

AC.L2-3.1.17.proof: WPA3-Enterprise with RADIUS authentication deployed. 802.1X for wireless access. Wireless IDS monitors for rogue access points. Configuration documented in SSP.

AC.L2-3.1.18.proof: MDM deployed for company-issued mobile devices. Personal device restrictions documented in policy. Not all mobile device types are fully managed.
AC.L2-3.1.18.plan: Extend MDM enrollment to all mobile devices accessing CUI. Implement mobile device compliance checking.

AC.L2-3.1.19.proof: Full device encryption enabled on all company-issued mobile devices via MDM. FIPS-validated encryption modules used. CUI containers encrypted on approved BYOD devices.

AC.L2-3.1.20.proof: External system connections limited by firewall rules. Some verification of external system security posture performed. Not consistently documented.
AC.L2-3.1.20.plan: Implement formal interconnection security agreements (ISAs) for all external system connections handling CUI.

AC.L2-3.1.21.proof: Portable storage device policy exists but enforcement is inconsistent on external systems. USB restrictions applied on internal systems only.
AC.L2-3.1.21.plan: Implement technical controls to prevent CUI transfer to portable storage on all systems. Document in SSP and update POAM.

AC.L2-3.1.22.proof: Publicly accessible systems segregated from CUI-processing systems. Content review process before public posting. CUI marking checks automated for outbound content.

AT.L2-3.2.1.proof: Annual security awareness training covers CUI handling requirements and NIST 800-171 obligations. Training completion tracked in LMS. All personnel completed training within 30 days of onboarding.

AT.L2-3.2.2.proof: Role-based training provided for system administrators and security personnel. Training materials cover CMMC-specific requirements. Advanced training for incident responders.
AT.L2-3.2.2.plan: Develop formal role-based training curriculum aligned with NICE framework. Track training effectiveness metrics.

AT.L2-3.2.3.proof: Insider threat awareness included in annual security training. Basic indicators of insider threat covered. No dedicated insider threat training program.
AT.L2-3.2.3.plan: Implement dedicated insider threat awareness training module with scenario-based exercises.

AU.L2-3.3.1.proof: Audit logging enabled on all CUI systems per SSP requirements. Logs capture user activity, system events, and security-relevant actions. Log retention meets NIST 800-171 requirements.

AU.L2-3.3.2.proof: Unique user IDs assigned to all users. Audit records include user identity, timestamp, action, and outcome. User activity traceable through centralized logging.

AU.L2-3.3.3.proof: Logged events defined in SSP. Periodic review of log event categories conducted. Not all system types have been reviewed for logging completeness.
AU.L2-3.3.3.plan: Conduct comprehensive audit log review across all CUI systems. Update logged event categories based on current threat landscape.

AU.L2-3.3.4.proof: Audit logging process failure alerts configured in SIEM. Automated notifications sent to security team when log collection fails. Alert response procedures documented.

AU.L2-3.3.5.proof: SIEM deployed with basic correlation rules. Audit log analysis performed for investigations. Automated correlation across all log sources not fully implemented.
AU.L2-3.3.5.plan: Implement advanced SIEM correlation rules covering MITRE ATT&CK techniques relevant to CUI protection. Add POAM item.

AU.L2-3.3.6.proof: SIEM provides audit record reduction and report generation. Pre-built dashboards for common audit queries. On-demand reporting capability available to security analysts.

AU.L2-3.3.7.proof: NTP synchronization configured across all systems. Authoritative time source (NIST time servers) configured. Clock synchronization monitored and alerting configured for drift.

AU.L2-3.3.8.proof: Audit logs stored on dedicated log management infrastructure. Access restricted to security team. Log integrity protected via write-once storage. Documented in SSP.

AU.L2-3.3.9.proof: Audit logging configuration restricted to security administrators. Changes to audit configuration require change management approval. Documented in SSP.

CM.L2-3.4.1.proof: Baseline configurations documented in SSP for all CUI system types. Hardware and software inventories maintained. Configuration baselines updated with each major change.

CM.L2-3.4.2.proof: Security configuration settings based on DISA STIGs applied to most systems. Not all system types have STIG compliance verified. Compliance scanning partially deployed.
CM.L2-3.4.2.plan: Deploy automated STIG compliance scanning across all CUI systems. Remediate identified deviations.

CM.L2-3.4.3.proof: Change management process documented. Changes tracked in ticketing system with approval workflow. Change log maintained for all CUI systems.

CM.L2-3.4.4.proof: Security impact analysis performed for major changes. Not consistently performed for all changes to CUI systems. Analysis template exists but not enforced.
CM.L2-3.4.4.plan: Mandate security impact analysis for all changes to CUI-processing systems. Integrate into change management workflow.

CM.L2-3.4.5.proof: Access restrictions for change implementation documented. Only authorized personnel can implement changes to production systems. Physical and logical access controls enforce restrictions.

CM.L2-3.4.6.proof: Least functionality principle applied to servers (unnecessary services disabled). Workstation configurations partially hardened. Full implementation across all system types pending.
CM.L2-3.4.6.plan: Complete least functionality implementation for all CUI endpoints. Document approved software and services list.

CM.L2-3.4.7.proof: Unnecessary services, ports, and protocols disabled per DISA STIG guidance. Firewall rules restrict to essential communications. Port scan verification performed quarterly.

CM.L2-3.4.8.proof: Application whitelisting deployed on servers. Workstation application control partially implemented. Blacklisting for known malicious software in place.
CM.L2-3.4.8.plan: Complete application whitelisting deployment on all CUI workstations. Update SSP and POAM.

CM.L2-3.4.9.proof: User software installation restricted via GPO on managed systems. Some exceptions for development environments. Monitoring of user-installed software not comprehensive.
CM.L2-3.4.9.plan: Implement automated monitoring and alerting for unauthorized software installations on CUI systems.

IA.L2-3.5.1.proof: All users, processes, and devices uniquely identified in Active Directory. Device certificates for network access. Service accounts cataloged and managed.

IA.L2-3.5.2.proof: Authentication required for all system access. AD authentication for domain resources. Local authentication for standalone systems. Authentication documented in SSP.

IA.L2-3.5.3.proof: MFA implemented for all remote access and privileged accounts. Hardware tokens issued for CUI system access. MFA solution uses FIPS-validated cryptography.

IA.L2-3.5.4.proof: Replay-resistant authentication mechanisms deployed for VPN access. Not all network authentication scenarios use replay-resistant mechanisms.
IA.L2-3.5.4.plan: Implement replay-resistant authentication across all CUI network access points. Document in SSP.

IA.L2-3.5.5.proof: Identifier reuse prevention configured in AD. Disabled accounts retained for 90 days before deletion. Unique identifier policy documented in SSP.

IA.L2-3.5.6.proof: Accounts disabled after 90 days of inactivity per GPO. Automated scripts check for inactive accounts weekly. Process documented in SSP.

IA.L2-3.5.7.proof: Password policy enforces minimum 14 characters, complexity requirements, and character change requirements. GPO enforced across all domain-joined systems. Compliant with NIST 800-171.

IA.L2-3.5.8.proof: Password history of 24 previous passwords enforced via GPO. Prevents reuse of recent passwords. Documented in SSP.

IA.L2-3.5.9.proof: Temporary passwords set to expire on first use. Password reset process requires identity verification. Temporary password procedures documented in SSP.

IA.L2-3.5.10.proof: Passwords stored using salted SHA-256 hashing. Transmission encrypted via TLS 1.2+. FIPS-validated cryptographic modules used. Documented in SSP.

IA.L2-3.5.11.proof: Authentication feedback obscured on all login screens (masked password fields). Error messages do not reveal whether username or password was incorrect.

IR.L2-3.6.1.proof: Incident response plan documented covering preparation, detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery. Plan addresses CUI-specific incident handling requirements.

IR.L2-3.6.2.proof: Incident tracking in ticketing system. Reports generated for internal management. External reporting procedures documented but not tested for DoD notification requirements.
IR.L2-3.6.2.plan: Validate DIBCAC notification procedures through tabletop exercise. Document DoD reporting timelines in IR plan.

IR.L2-3.6.3.proof: IR capability testing limited to informal discussions. No formal tabletop exercises or simulations conducted specific to CUI compromise scenarios.
IR.L2-3.6.3.plan: Schedule quarterly IR tabletop exercises focused on CUI breach scenarios. Engage external IR firm for annual full-scale exercise.
IR.L2-3.6.3.notes: POAM item #35 - critical gap for CMMC assessment.

MA.L2-3.7.1.proof: Maintenance schedule documented for CUI system components. Maintenance activities logged in asset management system. Performed by authorized personnel per SSP.

MA.L2-3.7.2.proof: Maintenance tools identified and controlled. Some tools not formally approved or tracked. Maintenance personnel use organization-provided tools where possible.
MA.L2-3.7.2.plan: Implement formal maintenance tool approval and tracking process. Document in SSP.

MA.L2-3.7.3.proof: Equipment sanitization procedures documented before off-site maintenance. CUI removal verified before equipment leaves controlled area. Sanitization records maintained.

MA.L2-3.7.4.proof: Diagnostic media scanned before use on production systems. Scanning process informal and not consistently applied across all maintenance scenarios.
MA.L2-3.7.4.plan: Formalize diagnostic media scanning procedure. Maintain approved media inventory.

MA.L2-3.7.5.proof: Remote maintenance requires VPN with MFA. Sessions terminated upon completion. Remote maintenance sessions logged and monitored. Documented in SSP.

MA.L2-3.7.6.proof: Escort procedures for maintenance personnel without required clearance. Supervision requirements documented. Maintenance activities observed and logged by authorized personnel.

MP.L2-3.8.1.proof: CUI media stored in locked cabinets in access-controlled areas. Digital media encrypted. Media inventory maintained. Storage locations documented in SSP.

MP.L2-3.8.2.proof: Access to CUI media restricted to authorized personnel. Access lists maintained. Media access logged. Authorization required before CUI media access.

MP.L2-3.8.3.proof: Media sanitization procedures follow NIST 800-88 guidance. Sanitization records maintained. Verification performed post-sanitization. Approved sanitization methods documented in SSP.

MP.L2-3.8.4.proof: CUI marking requirements understood but not consistently applied to all media types. Banner pages for printed CUI implemented. Digital file marking partially automated.
MP.L2-3.8.4.plan: Implement automated CUI marking for digital files. Train all personnel on media marking requirements.

MP.L2-3.8.5.proof: Media transport procedures exist for internal movements. External transport of CUI media uses encrypted containers. Accountability tracking during transport is informal.
MP.L2-3.8.5.plan: Implement formal media tracking system with chain-of-custody documentation for all CUI media transport.

MP.L2-3.8.6.proof: FIPS-validated encryption used for CUI on digital media during transport. Encrypted USB drives issued for authorized CUI transport. Documented in SSP.

MP.L2-3.8.7.proof: Removable media policy exists restricting use on CUI systems. Technical controls partially deployed. USB port blocking not enforced on all endpoints.
MP.L2-3.8.7.plan: Deploy USB device control on all CUI endpoints. Whitelist only approved encrypted USB devices. Update POAM.

MP.L2-3.8.8.proof: Policy states portable storage devices must have identifiable owners. Enforcement relies on user awareness. No technical verification mechanism.
MP.L2-3.8.8.plan: Implement device registration for all portable storage devices used with CUI systems.

MP.L2-3.8.9.proof: Backup CUI encrypted at storage locations. Backup media stored in access-controlled areas. Backup encryption uses FIPS-validated modules. Documented in SSP.

PE.L2-3.10.1.proof: Physical access restricted via badge-based access control system. CUI processing areas in controlled zones. Authorized personnel list maintained and reviewed quarterly.

PE.L2-3.10.2.proof: CCTV monitoring at facility entry points and CUI processing areas. Intrusion detection system for after-hours protection. Physical security monitoring procedures documented.

PE.L2-3.10.3.proof: Visitor management system with pre-registration requirement. Visitors escorted at all times in CUI areas. Visitor logs maintained for 1 year.

PE.L2-3.10.4.proof: Physical access logs generated by badge system. Logs retained for 1 year. Monthly review of access logs for anomalies. Documented in SSP.

PE.L2-3.10.5.proof: Physical access devices (badges, keys) inventoried and tracked. Lost/stolen badge procedures documented. Badge deactivation within 4 hours of report.

PE.L2-3.10.6.proof: Telework security guidelines exist. VPN and endpoint security required. Physical safeguarding of CUI at alternate work sites addressed in policy but not verified.
PE.L2-3.10.6.plan: Implement telework security verification checklist. Conduct periodic assessments of alternate work site safeguards.

PS.L2-3.9.1.proof: Background screening conducted for all personnel with CUI access. Screening criteria documented and aligned with position sensitivity levels. Records maintained in HR system.

PS.L2-3.9.2.proof: System access revoked within 24 hours of termination. Transfer procedures include access review and modification. Offboarding checklist includes CUI access revocation. Documented in SSP.

RA.L2-3.11.1.proof: Risk assessment conducted annually for CUI systems. Risk register maintained with identified threats, vulnerabilities, and risk ratings. Results inform SSP updates.

RA.L2-3.11.2.proof: Vulnerability scanning conducted monthly for CUI systems. Scan coverage does not include all network segments. Results tracked but remediation SLAs not consistently met.
RA.L2-3.11.2.plan: Increase scan frequency to weekly. Extend coverage to all CUI network segments. Enforce remediation SLAs.

RA.L2-3.11.3.proof: Vulnerability remediation prioritized by CVSS score. Critical vulnerabilities addressed within 30 days. Remediation tracking exists but not integrated with risk assessment process.
RA.L2-3.11.3.plan: Integrate vulnerability remediation with risk assessment process. Reduce critical vulnerability remediation to 14-day SLA.

CA.L2-3.12.1.proof: Security control assessments performed annually. Assessment methodology based on NIST 800-171A. Not all controls have been fully assessed with documented evidence.
CA.L2-3.12.1.plan: Complete comprehensive control assessment covering all 110 NIST 800-171 requirements. Document assessment results.

CA.L2-3.12.2.proof: Plan of Action and Milestones (POAM) maintained with identified deficiencies. Target remediation dates assigned. Monthly POAM review with management.

CA.L2-3.12.3.proof: Some security controls monitored continuously via automated tools. Manual control monitoring performed quarterly. Not all controls have ongoing monitoring mechanisms.
CA.L2-3.12.3.plan: Implement continuous monitoring program covering all NIST 800-171 security controls.

CA.L2-3.12.4.proof: System Security Plan (SSP) documented per NIST 800-171 requirements. SSP describes system boundaries, environment, security implementation, and interconnections. Updated annually.

SC.L2-3.13.1.proof: Network perimeter monitored and controlled via firewall and IDS/IPS. Internal boundary controls between CUI and non-CUI segments. Network architecture documented in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.2.proof: Defense-in-depth architecture implemented. Secure development principles followed for internal applications. Not all legacy systems designed with security architecture principles.
SC.L2-3.13.2.plan: Develop architectural security requirements for legacy system modernization. Prioritize CUI-handling legacy systems.

SC.L2-3.13.3.proof: User interface separated from system management functions. Administrative interfaces on separate management network. Documented in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.4.proof: Controls for shared system resources partially implemented. Memory and storage isolation configured for virtualized environments. Covert channel analysis not performed.
SC.L2-3.13.4.plan: Conduct covert channel analysis for shared CUI processing environments.

SC.L2-3.13.5.proof: DMZ deployed for public-facing components. Logical separation between public and internal networks. Firewall rules enforce segmentation. Documented in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.6.proof: Default-deny firewall policy implemented. Allowed traffic explicitly permitted by rule. Firewall rules reviewed quarterly. Documented in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.7.proof: Split tunneling prohibited in VPN configuration. Policy documented. Technical enforcement via VPN client configuration. Not verified across all remote access scenarios.
SC.L2-3.13.7.plan: Verify split tunneling prevention across all remote access methods including mobile devices.

SC.L2-3.13.8.proof: TLS 1.2+ enforced for all CUI transmissions. FIPS-validated cryptographic modules used. VPN encryption using AES-256. Cryptographic implementation documented in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.9.proof: Network session termination configured for inactivity. TCP connection timeouts set per security baseline. VPN session limits enforced. Documented in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.10.proof: Cryptographic key management procedures exist but not fully documented. Key lifecycle management partially automated. Key storage in HSM for critical keys.
SC.L2-3.13.10.plan: Document comprehensive key management procedures per NIST 800-57. Automate key rotation for all CUI encryption keys.

SC.L2-3.13.11.proof: FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules used for CUI protection. Certificate of validation on file. Module inventory maintained. Documented in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.12.proof: Collaborative computing devices (video conferencing) not formally addressed in CUI areas. Basic awareness of activation risks.
SC.L2-3.13.12.plan: Develop policy for collaborative computing devices in CUI areas. Implement technical controls where feasible.

SC.L2-3.13.13.proof: Mobile code restrictions configured in web browsers. Java and ActiveX controls restricted. Policy documented but technical enforcement not comprehensive.
SC.L2-3.13.13.plan: Implement comprehensive mobile code restrictions across all CUI endpoints.

SC.L2-3.13.14.proof: VoIP deployed but security controls specific to VoIP not formally documented or assessed for CUI conversations.
SC.L2-3.13.14.plan: Assess VoIP security controls. Implement encryption for VoIP traffic carrying CUI. Document in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.15.proof: Session authenticity protected via TLS mutual authentication for critical sessions. Session tokens use cryptographic integrity protection. Documented in SSP.

SC.L2-3.13.16.proof: CUI at rest encrypted using AES-256 with FIPS-validated modules. Full disk encryption on all CUI endpoints. Database encryption for CUI data stores. Documented in SSP.

SI.L2-3.14.1.proof: Flaw remediation process documented. Patch management program for CUI systems. Critical patches applied within 30 days. Flaw identification from vulnerability scans and vendor advisories.

SI.L2-3.14.2.proof: Endpoint protection deployed on all CUI systems. Real-time malware detection with automated quarantine. Multiple detection engines. Definitions updated automatically.

SI.L2-3.14.3.proof: Subscribed to US-CERT, CISA alerts, and vendor security advisories. Alerts reviewed and assessed for impact to CUI systems. Response actions documented.

SI.L2-3.14.4.proof: Malware protection mechanisms auto-update from vendor. Update status monitored centrally. Alerts generated for systems with outdated definitions. Documented in SSP.

SI.L2-3.14.5.proof: Weekly full system scans configured on all CUI endpoints. Real-time scanning for files from external sources. Scan results reviewed and anomalies investigated.

SI.L2-3.14.6.proof: Network monitoring deployed at perimeter. IDS/IPS monitors inbound and outbound traffic. Internal network monitoring limited to key segments only.
SI.L2-3.14.6.plan: Extend network monitoring to all CUI network segments. Implement internal traffic analysis for lateral movement detection.

SI.L2-3.14.7.proof: Unauthorized system use detection based on login anomalies and failed authentication monitoring. UEBA capabilities limited. Detection of sophisticated unauthorized use needs improvement.
SI.L2-3.14.7.plan: Implement UEBA solution for CUI systems. Define behavioral baselines and anomaly thresholds.
